parsed.summary: This clause states that Philips is giving up the right to receive a full refund for non-conforming Products if the Products are returned in exchange for a full refund.
parsed.rights:
1. Philips agrees to provide a full refund to the purchaser for any non-conforming Products returned in exchange for a full refund.

2. The purchaser agrees to return the non-conforming Products to Philips within 30 days of the date of delivery.

3. The purchaser agrees to pay the full purchase price for the non-conforming Products.
parsed.analogy:
Suppose a customer returns a TV for a refund because it has a cracked screen. The customer would be entitled to a full refund, which means they would be getting their money back. In this case, the customer would be giving up their right to receive a refund for the cracked screen.

Note: The real-world analogy is not intended to be a legal or contractual interpretation, but rather a simple explanation for a common scenario.
parsed.raw:
Plain-Language Summary: This clause states that Philips is giving up the right to receive a full refund for non-conforming Products if the Products are returned in exchange for a full refund.

Rights & Obligations:

1. Philips agrees to provide a full refund to the purchaser for any non-conforming Products returned in exchange for a full refund.

2. The purchaser agrees to return the non-conforming Products to Philips within 30 days of the date of delivery.

3. The purchaser agrees to pay the full purchase price for the non-conforming Products.

Real-World Analogy: Suppose a customer returns a TV for a refund because it has a cracked screen. The customer would be entitled to a full refund, which means they would be getting their money back. In this case, the customer would be giving up their right to receive a refund for the cracked screen.

Note: The real-world analogy is not intended to be a legal or contractual interpretation, but rather a simple explanation for a common scenario.

parsed.summary: This clause is a warranty that the Company will provide products that meet the standard of merchantability and general commercial salability within 12 months of shipment. The Company warrants that the products have been manufactured, labeled, and packaged in accordance with all applicable laws and will be handled, stored, and shipped in compliance with these standards. The Customer's exclusive remedy for a breach of this warranty is the replacement of any product that fails to meet these standards.
parsed.rights:
1. The Customer agrees that the Company shall be entitled to indemnify and hold harmless the Customer from any and all claims, damages, costs, or expenses arising out of or related to the warranty provided by this clause.
2. The Customer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Company from any and all claims, damages, costs, or expenses arising out of or related to any breach of this warranty.
3. The Customer agrees to accept this warranty and waive any rights it may have under any other warranty, guarantee, or indemnification clause.
parsed.analogy: Imagine a situation where you purchase a car from a dealership that claims it has a warranty to cover any defects or issues with the car for a certain period of time. If you later discover that the car has a problem that is not covered by the warranty, you may have a claim against the dealership for the cost of repairing or replacing the car.
parsed.raw:
Plain-Language Summary: This clause is a warranty that the Company will provide products that meet the standard of merchantability and general commercial salability within 12 months of shipment. The Company warrants that the products have been manufactured, labeled, and packaged in accordance with all applicable laws and will be handled, stored, and shipped in compliance with these standards. The Customer's exclusive remedy for a breach of this warranty is the replacement of any product that fails to meet these standards.

Rights & Obligations:
1. The Customer agrees that the Company shall be entitled to indemnify and hold harmless the Customer from any and all claims, damages, costs, or expenses arising out of or related to the warranty provided by this clause.
2. The Customer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Company from any and all claims, damages, costs, or expenses arising out of or related to any breach of this warranty.
3. The Customer agrees to accept this warranty and waive any rights it may have under any other warranty, guarantee, or indemnification clause.

Real-World Analogy:
Imagine a situation where you purchase a car from a dealership that claims it has a warranty to cover any defects or issues with the car for a certain period of time. If you later discover that the car has a problem that is not covered by the warranty, you may have a claim against the dealership for the cost of repairing or replacing the car.
